items: Identify a single executive sponsor for AI initiatives
Audit current data landscape — what exists, where, and in what quality
Map top 5 most repetitive workflows across functions
Shortlist 2–3 potential AI use cases based on effort vs. impact
Clean and consolidate data in one priority area
Evaluate 2–3 off-the-shelf AI tools for your top use case
Define minimum governance requirements (data handling, privacy)
Run a small proof-of-concept with clear success criteria
Hire or contract a junior data / AI specialist
Formalize an AI evaluation framework for future initiatives
Expand the validated pilot to a second business function
Establish quarterly AI review cadence with leadership
Define clear ownership for AI initiatives within the organization
Consolidate existing AI experiments into a single priority list
Assess data quality and accessibility gaps in priority areas
Define 1–2 high-value use cases with measurable success criteria
Run a structured AI pilot with KPIs and timeline
Define basic governance checkpoints for AI initiatives
Train a small cross-functional working group on AI literacy
Create a simple ROI model for the first active use case
Scale the validated pilot into production use
Define a repeatable delivery model for future AI projects
Standardize data governance and risk review processes
Evaluate build / buy / partner options systematically
Review current AI portfolio — what's working, what's not
Identify the next high-impact function for AI expansion
Strengthen governance processes for production AI systems
Define a measurement framework across all active AI initiatives

Create change management playbook for AI rollouts

Build reusable AI infrastructure and data pipelines

Develop competitive advantage use cases beyond efficiency
Optimize the AI portfolio — identify underperforming initiatives
